CreateCfw.name: CFW name, up to 65 characters, consisting of digits, letters, and underscores
CreateCfw.type: CFW type; value 1 indicates application-type firewall
CreateCfw.border: CFW protection boundary; values 0, 1, 2 indicate internet boundary, VPC boundary, and NAT boundary respectively; default is internet boundary
CreateCfw.cfwRules.protocol: Protocol type; valid values: [ TCP | UDP | ICMP | ALL ]
CreateCfw.cfwRules.direction: Traffic direction; valid values: [ in | out ]; NAT boundary firewall supports outbound rules only, inbound traffic is allowed by default
CreateCfw.cfwRules.sourceAddress: Source IP address or CIDR block, e.g. 180.76.1.0/24 or 180.76.1.1; 0.0.0.0/0 means all
CreateCfw.cfwRules.destAddress: Destination IP address or CIDR block, e.g. 180.76.1.0/24 or 180.76.1.1; 0.0.0.0/0 means all
CreateCfw.cfwRules.sourcePort: Source port; integer or range between 0-65535, e.g. 8080 or 8080-8090; 0-65535 means all
CreateCfw.cfwRules.destPort: Destination port; integer or range between 0-65535, e.g. 8080 or 8080-8090; 0-65535 means all
CreateCfw.cfwRules.action: Policy action; valid values: [ allow | deny | observe ]
